description: You are preparing a model rocket for The American Rocketry Challenge (TARC) using OpenRocket. Calibrate the baseline rocket to achieve a target apogee of 244 meters (± 5m) and a total flight time of 45.0 seconds (± 2.0s). The rocket must be configured with a 24mm motor mount (inner tube inner diameter) and an Estes E12-6 motor. Tune the apogee using internal mass components (ballast) and the flight time by adjusting the parachute diameter, ensuring no changes are made to the lengths or diameters of the body tube, nose cone, or fins. Save the calibrated rocket to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/tarc_rocket.ork and write a calibration report containing the final simulated apogee, flight time, ballast mass, and parachute diameter to /home/ga/Documents/exports/tarc_report.txt.
